decisions.decision: Fix 50 confirmed findings from 4-agent deep audit across scripts, commands, docs, and architecture
decisions.rationale: Second systematic audit after workflow-audit-fixes; covers deeper structural and architectural issues

decisions.decision: Organize into 4-6 phases by risk: data integrity first, then state machine, then commands, then docs
decisions.rationale: HIGH findings in memory engine and validation must land before cosmetic fixes

decisions.decision: Phase transition validation and state/phase unification are core improvements
decisions.rationale: Current system allows nonsensical state combos and backward phase movement

decisions.decision: Standardize Step 0 structure and add error recovery paths
decisions.rationale: Inconsistent numbering and missing failure handling across discuss/plan/implement/review/ship

constraints: Must not break existing memory DB (migration required for schema changes)
Changes to commands/skills must pass workflow_validate.py
Agent team specialization fix must be config-only (no new agent files needed)
openQuestions: Should status column be deprecated in favor of state-only? (migration risk)
Should phase transitions be strict (enforced) or advisory (warned)?
How much CI/CD integration is in scope vs future work?
Should rollback/undo be a full feature or just phase revert?
